segments.title: Washington Treaty subjects in the series
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke maps the Washington Treaty subjects covered by the equitable-treaty series, from capital ships and carriers to cruisers and fortifications.
segments.body: This opening schedule maps the Washington Treaty portion of the equitable-treaty series across capital ships, aircraft carriers, other vessel categories, and fortifications. Dr. Clarke notes that the capital-ship and carrier installments are already available before identifying the remaining subjects. He then previews an Ultimate Admiral: Dreadnoughts episode built around the cruiser he would design under the treaty framework. The sequence gives viewers a compact route from written limits to the practical choices involved in creating a treaty cruiser, followed by an overall Washington settlement.

evidence.note: The transcript lists the Washington Treaty capital-ship, carrier, other-ships, fortifications, and cruiser-design installments.

segments.title: London Treaty subjects and fleet categories
segments.summary: The outline previews how the London treaty discussions move across capital ships, carriers, cruisers, destroyers, sloops, and fortifications.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke lays out a category-by-category route through the London naval treaties, beginning with capital ships and aircraft carriers before moving into cruisers, destroyers, sloops, and other vessels. The schedule also places the Pensacola class at the start of a separate United States cruiser series, providing a historical design alongside the counterfactual treaty discussions. A possible Ultimate Admiral: Dreadnoughts interlude would set capital ships, cruisers, or destroyers against one another and connect paper fleet limits with simulated combat. The sequence ends with fortifications and an overall equitable settlement, emphasizing that treaty design links many parts of fleet structure rather than treating each warship category in isolation.

evidence.note: The transcript enumerates the London Treaty installments and the fleet categories each will cover.
